special_mechanics.name: Ball Physics
special_mechanics.description: Pinball ball movement governed by realistic physics simulation. Ball moves according to table gravity, bumper collisions, ramp angles, and flipper mechanics.

physics_values.note: Designed to simulate realistic pinball table physics

special_mechanics.name: Flipper Mechanics
special_mechanics.description: Player controls flippers to launch and guide the pinball on the playing field

special_mechanics.name: Table Elements
special_mechanics.description: All table elements (bumpers, ramps, targets, drains) designed to be theoretically constructible in physical reality

tier_notes: Tier 3: No quantified movement parameters available in sources. This is a pinball simulation game, not a character action game, so traditional player movement metrics (walk speed, jump velocity, gravity) do not apply. Documentation confirms the game uses a realistic physics model for ball movement but provides no specific numerical values for physics parameters.
sources_used: Wikipedia - Pinball Dreams (gameplay and development sections mentioning ball physics)
The Cutting Room Floor - Pinball Dreams (SNES) (game info and platform details)
